citations.narrative:
The records for animals on hand are incomplete. There is no documentation of the disposition of 1 sheep, 5 goats, 5
chickens , and1 pig that was present at the last inspection in May 2025. Also, there is no information in the records for 2
newly acquired sheep.
Acquisition and disposition records are necessary to accurately account for and track animals used in regulated activities
and to ensure their humane care. The facility shall make, keep and maintain records which fully disclose all the required
information concerning animals purchased or otherwise acquired and disposed of as outlined in this subpart 2.75 (b)(1).
Correct by May 31, 2026

At the time of the inspection, the license was not able to provide a documented contingency plan for the facility. Without
adequate contingency planning, the animals at the facility may not be provided with appropriate protection and care in the
event of an disaster or emergency.
The licensee must develop, document, and follow an appropriate contingency plan, in accordance with 2.134(a)(1) to (4),
to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the event of an
emergency or disaster (one which could reasonably be anticipated and expected to be detrimental to the good health and
well-being of the animals in their possession). Correct by June 15, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with licensee.

narrative:
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The records for animals on hand are incomplete. There is no documentation of the disposition of 1 sheep, 5 goats, 5 
chickens , and1 pig that was present at the last inspection in May 2025. Also, there is no information in the records for 2 
newly acquired sheep.  
 
Acquisition and disposition records are necessary to accurately account for and track animals used in regulated activities 
and to ensure their humane care. The facility shall make, keep and maintain records which fully disclose all the required 
information concerning animals purchased or otherwise acquired and disposed of as outlined in this subpart 2.75 (b)(1). 
Correct by May 31, 2026 
2.134(a) 
   
Contingency planning. 
At the time of the inspection, the license was not able to provide a documented contingency plan for the facility. Without 
adequate contingency planning, the animals at the facility may not be provided with appropriate protection and care in the 
event of an disaster or emergency. 
 
The licensee must develop, document, and follow an appropriate contingency plan, in accordance with 2.134(a)(1) to (4), 
to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the event of an 
emergency or disaster (one which could reasonably be anticipated and expected to be detrimental to the good health and 
well-being of the animals in their possession). Correct by June 15, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with licensee.
